name: Hoz de Pelegrina and Gollorio Waterfall Trail
name_original: Hoz de Pelegrina y cascada de Gollorio — circular por el Parque Natural Barranco del Río Dulce
description: Hike a moderate 4.4-mile loop through Pelegrina Gorge and past Gollorio Waterfall in Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park.
region_intro: Waterfall hiking trails in Barranco Del Río Dulce are characterized by deep canyons and gorges carved by the Dulce River through Mesozoic limestone and dolomites. The landscape features impressive cliffs, rock walls, and a gallery forest along the riverbanks. Beyond the main canyon, the park includes Alcarrian limestone plateaus and small tributary valleys, supporting diverse flora such as holm oaks and Portuguese oaks. Best waterfall hiking trails in Barranco Del Río Dulce The most popular waterfall hiking route is Pelegrina Gorge and Gollorio Waterfall - circular route through the Barranco del Rio Dulce Natural Park, a 4.4 miles (7.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 17 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Gollorio Waterfall and the rugged gorge. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pelegrina Castle – Gollorio Waterfall loop from Castillo de Pelegrina, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.8 km) path. This trail winds through the natural park, providing scenic views of the castle and the waterfall. Local hikers also love the Gollorio Waterfall loop from Torremocha del Campo, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail leading through the Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park, often completed in about 1 hour 24 minutes. Hiking in Barranco Del Río Dulce is defined by deep canyons, limestone formations, and the flowing Dulce River. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels, with 7 moderate and 1 difficult trail available. The routes in Barranco Del Río Dulce are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Barranco Del Río Dulce's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many waterfall hiking trails are available in Barranco Del Río Dulce?
A: There are 8 dedicated waterfall hiking trails in Barranco Del Río Dulce. These routes offer diverse experiences, from moderate walks to more challenging treks, all leading to or offering views of the region's waterfalls.

Q: What is the best time of year to visit the waterfalls in Barranco Del Río Dulce?
A: The Gollorio Waterfall, a prominent feature in the park, is most impressive from autumn to spring when rainfall replenishes its water sources. Spring and autumn are generally recommended for hiking in the park due to pleasant weather and vibrant natural activity, making them ideal for experiencing the waterfalls.

Q: Are there any circular waterfall hiking routes in Barranco Del Río Dulce?
A: Yes, several circular routes allow you to experience the waterfalls. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025428280/pelegrina-gorge-and-gollorio-waterfall-circular-route-through-the-barranco-del-rio-dulce-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pelegrina Gorge and Gollorio Waterfall - circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, offering views of the Gollorio Waterfall and the rugged gorge.

Q: What can I see along the waterfall hiking trails besides the waterfalls?
A: Beyond the waterfalls, you'll encounter the deep canyons and impressive cliffs carved by the Dulce River. Many trails offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3399704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pelegrina Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5306728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Dulce Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. You might also spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3399716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Félix Rodríguez de la Fuente Shelter&lt;/a&gt;, a nod to the naturalist's legacy in the area.

Q: Are the waterfall hikes in Barranco Del Río Dulce suitable for families?
A: Many routes in the Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park are suitable for families, with some being practically flat and having minimal elevation changes. While specific 'easy' waterfall routes aren't listed, the park generally offers options for various fitness levels. Consider routes starting from villages like Pelegrina or Aragosa, which often have amenities.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of waterfall hikes in this region?
A: The waterfall hikes in Barranco Del Río Dulce range from moderate to difficult. Out of the 8 available routes, 7 are classified as moderate, and 1 is considered difficult. This means most trails are accessible to hikers with a reasonable level of fitness, while some offer a greater challenge.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter near the waterfall trails?
A: The park is a haven for wildlife. Near the rocky areas and cliffs, you might observe griffon vultures, Egyptian vultures, and even Bonelli's eagles. The riverbanks and woodlands are home to roe deer, wild boar, and otters. Birdwatching is particularly rewarding from viewpoints overlooking the gorge.

Q: Are there any long-distance waterfall hikes in Barranco Del Río Dulce?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer adventure, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14885966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gollorio Waterfall – Pelegrina Castle loop from Castillo de Pelegrina&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 10.7-mile (17.3 km) route that takes approximately 5 hours to complete, offering extensive views and a more demanding experience.

Q: What do other hikers say about the waterfall trails in Barranco Del Río Dulce?
A: The waterfall trails in Barranco Del Río Dulce are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning natural beauty, the impressive gorges, and the rewarding views of the Gollorio Waterfall.

Q: Are there facilities like picnic areas or water sources near the waterfall trails?
A: Yes, the park provides amenities in several villages. Recreational areas with picnic tables and some water fountains can be found in locations like Pelegrina, Aragosa, Mandayona, and Mirabueno, which are often starting points or accessible from the waterfall trails.

Q: Can I access the waterfall trails using public transport?
A: While the region is generally accessible, specific public transport options directly to all trailheads might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules to villages like Pelegrina or Sigüenza, which can serve as starting points for exploring the park and its waterfall trails.

Q: Is the Gollorio Waterfall visible year-round?
A: The Gollorio Waterfall is most prominent and impressive from autumn to spring, when the region receives more rainfall. During drier summer months, its flow may be significantly reduced or even cease, so visiting outside of summer is recommended for the best experience.
tips: Former camp of the team of the naturalist, creator and host of the "Man and the Earth" program.

Waterfall located in the bed of the Gollorio ravine, a tributary of the Dulce River, within the natural park of the Río Dulce Ravine. It is about fifty meters high and only appears from autumn to spring when the rains fill the sources that supply the bed of the ravine.
Although the ruins are few, it is worth contemplating the beauty of the Río Dulce valley at our feet, and understanding the name of the town, from the Latin pellus grinae, beautiful view.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Pelegrina Gorge and Gollorio Waterfall circular route?
faqs.answer: The most common starting point for this circular route is from the village of Pelegrina. You'll find parking available in or near the village, which provides convenient access to the trailhead for the Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered a moderate hike, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness. The terrain includes irregular paths with loose stones and slopes. You'll traverse riverside paths, cross the Dulce River, and navigate some areas that may require minor scrambling over rocks. There's also an 'equipped path' with a safety chain to reach the base of the Gollorio Waterfall, which can feel exposed.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Pelegrina Gorge and Gollorio Waterfall route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is from autumn to spring. The Gollorio Waterfall is seasonal and is most impressive after heavy rains, so visiting outside of the dry summer months is highly recommended for the best experience.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trails in Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed in the Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park. However, it's important to keep them on a leash at all times to protect the local wildlife, especially nesting birds, and to ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required for general hiking on this route within the Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable sights. You'll experience the dramatic geological formations of the Pelegrina Gorge, including impressive rock walls. Key highlights include the stunning Gollorio Waterfall, the historic Pelegrina Castle, and the Félix Rodríguez de la Fuente Shelter, which has historical ties to nature documentaries filmed in the park.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other long-distance paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route partially overlaps with sections of several well-known paths. You'll find it intersects with parts of the GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT, the Camino del Cid (Senderista), and the Camino de Santiago de la Lana.Senderista(Var.Sigüenza).

faqs.question: Is the Pelegrina Gorge and Gollorio Waterfall route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: While some sections of the Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park are suitable for families, the full circular route to the Gollorio Waterfall is described as moderate and includes irregular terrain and an exposed equipped path. It's best suited for families with older children who have good fitness and are sure-footed. There might be easier, shorter options within the park for younger children.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park is a protected zone renowned for its avian richness. You're likely to spot various raptors, including griffon vultures, Egyptian vultures, and short-toed eagles, nesting in the cliffs. The area is a fantastic spot for birdwatching.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trailhead in Pelegrina?
faqs.answer: Pelegrina is a small village, and while specific amenities can vary, you may find small shops or local establishments offering refreshments. It's always a good idea to check in advance or bring your own supplies, especially water and snacks, as options might be limited directly at the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the significance of Félix Rodríguez de la Fuente to this natural park?
faqs.answer: The Barranco del Río Dulce Natural Park gained significant fame as a filming location for the acclaimed Spanish naturalist Félix Rodríguez de la Fuente's nature documentaries, 'El Hombre y la Tierra.' Hikers can even see the Félix Rodríguez de la Fuente Shelter, which was used to store his filming equipment.

waypoints.name: Félix Rodríguez de la Fuente Shelter
